name: consent_natural
notes: Fresh UN-consented tenant (owner_inputs=false). A substantive message hits the VT-303 consent gate -> the deterministic consent_required_handler sends the 'reply ACTIVATE TEAM' enable prompt. A NATURAL 'yes go ahead' SHOULD grant consent, but current behavior only honors the EXACT enable keyword (pre_filter Rule a2) — so natural consent is ignored and the brain never engages. EXPECT FAIL until VT-583 lands. Run: `setup --owner-inputs false` (no --onboarded, no --journey — the consent gate runs BEFORE the journey is ever consulted, so no onboarding_journey/business_profile_draft state is needed here) then `script <tenant> consent_natural.json`. NOTE: consent_required_handler sends freeform WITHOUT a tenant_id, so its enable-prompt reply is NOT recorded to conversation_log (a VT-579 completeness gap) — that is why steps 1-2 set assert_no_silent=false and lean on run_status; step 3 is the load-bearing assertion (a brain reply IS conversation_log-logged, so its ABSENCE is the real, honest silent signal of the VT-583 gap). [2026-07-10: expected_fail DROPPED — the CL-2026-07-03 fluid-consent grant graduated this scenario; #51 pack showed XPASS on all 3 runs (natural 'yes go ahead' now grants + the journey proceeds).]

steps.message: I want to plan a Diwali campaign for my shop
steps.note: Un-consented substantive message -> consent_required_handler sends the enable prompt. run_status should be 'completed'. The prompt itself is not conversation_log-logged (VT-579 gap), so assert_no_silent is off here.

steps.message: yes go ahead, please enable it
steps.note: The NATURAL consent grant. Under current behavior this does NOT match the exact 'ACTIVATE TEAM' keyword, so owner_inputs stays false and the tenant is re-prompted (again un-logged). This is the VT-583 gap in action.

steps.message: great, now go plan that Diwali campaign
steps.note: If natural consent had been honored, the brain would engage and produce a conversation_log-logged manager reply. It does NOT (still gated) -> silent -> XFAIL. When VT-583 lands this flips to XPASS (flagged) — re-check the mark.
